mission.title: kubeflow: User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace: anonymous
mission.description: User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace: anonymous. This issue affects 10+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
Installed kubeflow 1.0.

steps.title: Apply the fix for User None is not authorized to list ... for namespace:
steps.description:
The logs from the jupyter-web-app:
```
"GET /api/namespaces/anonymous/notebooks HTTP/1.1" 200 -
2020-02-05 00:42:38,949 | kubeflow_jupyter.common.auth | WARNING | No user credentials were found! Make sure you have correctly set the USERID_HEADER in the Jupyter Web App's deployment.
```
The
```yaml
"GET /api/namespaces/anonymous/notebooks HTTP/1.1" 200 -
2020-02-05 00:42:38,949 | kubeflow_jupyter.common.auth | WARNING | No user credentials were found! Make sure you have correctly set the USERID_HEADER in the Jupyter Web App's deployment.
```

steps.title: Confirm User None is not authorized to list ... for is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
The logs from the jupyter-web-app:
```
"GET /api/namespaces/anonymous/notebooks HTTP/1.1" 200 -
2020-02-05 00:42:38,949 | kubeflow_jupyter.common.auth | WARNING | No user credentials were found! Make sure you have correctly set the USERID_HEADER in the Jupyter Web App's deployment.
codeSnippets:
"GET /api/namespaces/anonymous/notebooks HTTP/1.1" 200 -
2020-02-05 00:42:38,949 | kubeflow_jupyter.common.auth | WARNING | No user credentials were found! Make sure you have correctly set the USERID_HEADER in the Jupyter Web App's deployment.

apiVersion: networking.istio.io/v1alpha3
kind: EnvoyFilter
metadata:
  name: no-auth-filter
spec:
  workloadLabels:
    istio: ingressgateway
  filters:
  - filterConfig:
      inlineCode: |
        function envoy_on_response(response_handle)
          response_handle:headers():add("userid_header", "kubeflow-userid")
          response_handle:headers():add("kubeflow-userid", "anonymous")
        end
    filterName: envoy.lua
    filterType: HTTP
    insertPosition:
      index: FIRST
    listenerMatch:
      listenerType: GATEWAY

> GET /_/jupyter HTTP/1.1
.
.
< HTTP/1.1 200 OK
.
.
< userid_header: kubeflow-userid
< kubeflow-userid: anonymous
.
.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
